tile.title: Underwater Physics Models for Sonar-Vision Fusion
tile.content: Three physics models bridge the gap between acoustic sensing and optical imaging. (1) WaterColumnModel: Mackenzie equation (1981) for sound speed c = 1448.96 + 4.591T - 5.304e-2T² + 2.374e-4T³ + 1.340(S-35) + 1.630e-2D + 1.675e-7D² where T=temperature, S=salinity, D=depth. Sound speed increases with depth (pressure effect dominates thermocline). (2) Beer-Lambert light attenuation: per-channel RGB attenuation where red absorbs first (absorption coefficient ~0.4/m), green moderate (~0.1/m), blue penetrates deepest (~0.05/m). Result: underwater images are blue-green shifted, with red channel near-zero below 20m. (3) SonarBeamModel: range resolution = c/(2*f) where f=sonar frequency. 200kHz sonar → ~3.75mm range resolution. Beam footprint widens with depth: footprint = 2*depth*tan(beam_width/2).
